title: NanoClaw Adopts OneCLI Agent Vault
content: NanoClaw Adopts OneCLI Agent Vault | NanoClaw Blog NanoClaw is adopting OneCLI as its default credential and proxying layer. Every NanoClaw agent will access external services through OneCLI’s Agent Vault, a gateway that handles credential injection, access policies, and approvals so agents never hold raw API keys. NanoClaw already isolates every agent in its own Docker container. OneCLI’s Agent Vault gives you fine-grained controls over what those agents can access and how. How the integration works NanoClaw previously ran its own credential proxy that held every secret in memory. We replaced it with the @onecli-sh/sdk . When NanoClaw spins up a container, it calls applyContainerConfig() to route outbound HTTPS traffic through the OneCLI gateway, which injects the real credential: import { OneCLI } from '@onecli-sh/sdk' ; const onecli = new OneCLI ({ url: ONECLI_URL }); // Configure container to route through the gateway await onecli. applyContainerConfig (containerArgs, { agent: agentIdentifier, // per-agent credential policies }); Each NanoClaw agent group gets its own OneCLI agent identity, so your sales agent and support agent can have different credential policies. You register credentials once with onecli secrets create and the gateway matches outbound requests by host and path. Why this matters OpenClaw proved that people will hand over the keys to their email, their calendar, their code repos, their databases in order to get the value of an agent doing real work on their behalf. Millions of people did exactly that, and most of the time it works out fine. But when it doesn’t, the consequences are real. A director of AI alignment at Meta gave OpenClaw access to her email and explicitly told it not to take any action without her approval. The agent started mass-deleting emails anyway. She couldn’t stop it from her phone and had to physically run to her computer to kill the process. That story is what happens when agents operate without boundaries. The value of agents comes from giving them access to real systems and real data. An agent that can’t touch anything is just a chatbot. But an agent that can touch everything, with no policies, no rate limits, no approval flows, is a liability. The question is how you get the Claw unlock without the risk. Beyond secrets management Most people using agents today either hardcode API keys in environment variables or pull them from a secrets manager. Secrets managers like HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager solve storage, but they don’t solve what happens when the agent actually uses a credential. The agent fetches the key, and from that point on the key is in the agent’s environment, in its context, extractable via prompt injection. The vault protected the secret at rest, but once the agent has it, the vault is out of the picture. The Agent Vault sits between the agent and the services it calls. title_zh: NanoClaw 采用 OneCLI Agent Vault
summary_zh: NanoClaw采用OneCLI Agent Vault作为其默认凭证与代理层。所有NanoClaw agent将通过OneCLI的Agent Vault网关访问外部服务，该网关负责凭证注入、访问策略和审批流程，从而确保agent永不持有原始API密钥。NanoClaw本身已将每个agent隔离在独立的Docker容器中，而OneCLI的Agent Vault进一步提供了对agent访问权限和方式的细粒度控制。，集成工作原理方面，NanoClaw此前运行一个在内存中存储所有密钥的自有凭证代理。现在，团队使用@onecli-sh/sdk替代了原有方案。当NanoClaw启动容器时，会调用applyContainerConfig()函数，将出站HTTPS流量路由至OneCLI网关，由网关注入真实凭证。每个NanoClaw agent组都拥有独立的OneCLI agent身份，这意味着销售agent和支持agent可以配置不同的凭证策略。用户只需通过onecli secrets create注册一次凭证，网关便会根据请求的主机和路径进行匹配。，这一改进的重要性在于，OpenClaw的案例证明，用户为了获得agent代为处理实际工作的价值，愿意交出电子邮件、日历、代码仓库和数据库的访问权限。数百万人确实这样做了，且多数情况下运行良好。但一旦出现问题，后果是真实的。例如，Meta的一位AI对齐负责人曾授予OpenClaw其邮箱访问权，并明确要求未经批准不得执行任何操作，但该agent仍开始批量删除邮件。她无法通过手机阻止，只能跑回电脑终止进程。这正体现了无边界操作的agent可能带来的风险。，agent的价值源于其对真实系统和数据的访问能力。一个无法接触任何资源的agent仅是聊天机器人；而一个能接触一切、无策略、无限流、无审批流程的agent则构成潜在责任。核心问题在于如何在不引入风险的前提下实现“Claw解锁”。，超越传统密钥管理的是，当前大多数agent用户要么将API密钥硬编码在环境变量中，要么从密钥管理器（如HashiCorp Vault或AWS Secrets Manager）拉取。这些管理器解决了存储问题，但未解决agent实际使用凭证时的安全问题。一旦agent获取密钥，密钥便存在于其环境和上下文中，可能通过提示注入等方式被提取。传统保险库仅保护静态密钥，而Agent Vault则位于agent与其调用的服务之间。用户的凭证仍存储于原有位置，但原始密钥不会直接交给agent，从而在运行时持续保持控制。

title_zh: iPhone 17 Pro演示运行4000亿参数大语言模型
summary_zh: 近日有演示显示，iPhone 17 Pro 成功运行了一个参数量达 4000 亿的大型语言模型（LLM）。这一技术突破主要得益于苹果公司在其最新 A 系列芯片中集成的专用神经引擎和大幅提升的内存带宽，使得在移动设备端本地部署超大规模 AI 模型成为可能。，演示中，该 400B 参数模型在 iPhone 17 Pro 上实现了流畅的文本生成与对话交互，响应速度接近实时，展现了强大的端侧 AI 处理能力。关键技术细节包括：模型经过量化与压缩优化，以适应移动设备的存储与算力限制；同时利用了硬件加速的 Transformer 推理引擎，显著提升了计算效率。，这一进展的核心价值在于，它标志着高性能生成式 AI 正从云端向边缘设备迁移。用户无需依赖网络连接或云端 API，即可在手机本地运行接近 GPT-4 或 Claude 3 级别的 LLM，这不仅能提升响应速度和隐私安全性，也为离线场景下的复杂 AI 应用（如实时翻译、个人助理、内容创作）开辟了新路径。，此外，该演示也暗示了苹果未来在 AI 生态上的战略方向——通过深度融合硬件与软件，在 iPhone 等设备上构建强大的本地 AI 能力，可能减少对第三方云服务的依赖，并为其操作系统和应用程序带来更深度、更个性化的智能功能。

title: Show HN: Cq – Stack Overflow for AI coding agents
content: cq: Stack Overflow for Agents Side A: Turtles all the way down / Side B: Mo' tokens mo' problems If you've been around long enough in anything you start to see history repeating, fashion trends come back around, humanity makes the same mistakes. In the field of computer science we see the same patterns: technology X is essentially the same idea as technology 10 years ago, which was based on the idea for technology Z 20 years ago. Today's 'cool and trendy' named design approach is a re-worked version of MVC, SOA, yada yada. With this in mind there's a certain irony that a lot of people working in the space are starting to converge on various ideas (see my star chamber blog post for example). Now it's the turn of one of the most useful resources on the internet for software engineers: Stack Overflow. Born in 2008, peaking at over 200,000 questions a month by 2014. Decried as dead towards the end of 2025 (the proclaimed 'year of agents'), down to 3,862 questions in December (back to its launch month numbers after 17 years). The drop off started around the time ChatGPT launched. Who needs to share knowledge when ChatGPT/Claude/Gemini et al. "know everything"? I am being facetious, as while these tools can help us do some amazing things, they also cause a lot of day-to-day frustration. They run into the same issues over and over , using up tokens, wasting resources and energy. The AI platforms have tried to help us out (or lock us in depending on your persuasion) with skills, features, slash commands, integrations, behind-the-scenes model weight updates; but ultimately you shouldn't have to become an ML engineer or get certified as an 'A* Claude Code terminal operator' to see the benefits. Anyway, back to the story circa 2026: LLMs trained on the corpus of Stack Overflow LLMs via Agents committed matriphagy on Stack Overflow Agents run into the same issues over and over in isolation because their training data is stale etc. Agents now need their own Stack Overflow ... the cycle continues And yes, I chose that word deliberately. Matriphagy ; the offspring consuming the parent. Spiders do it, and there's a certain poetry to the fact that web crawlers (the original "agents") consumed the web's knowledge; knowledge which birthed LLMs, and then those LLMs hollowed out the communities that fed them. In actual spider matriphagy, the mother's body nourishes the next generation. Stack Overflow's corpus genuinely did nourish the LLMs. The question is whether the next generation builds something sustainable or just moves on to the next host. Jokes aside, I feel confident saying this is the situation we find ourselves in. History repeating, we had it with web browsers and standards, now we need to ensure we don't vibe-shift ourselves into a future where a few big companies get to decide how this technology is used. Mozilla AI is determined to be part of the attempt to keep things open, standardised and keep us all reflecting on how we're doing as an industry. AI
title_zh: Show HN：Cq——面向AI编程助手的Stack Overflow
summary_zh: 项目cq被定位为“面向AI编程智能体的Stack Overflow”。其背景源于当前AI开发中一个日益凸显的循环困境：以Stack Overflow为代表的传统开发者社区，其知识库滋养了早期大型语言模型（LLM）的训练，但随着ChatGPT、Claude、Gemini等AI工具的普及，开发者提问量急剧下降（例如Stack Overflow月提问量从2014年高峰期的20万跌至2025年12月的不足4000）。与此同时，基于这些LLM构建的AI智能体（Agents）在实际编码任务中，却因训练数据陈旧、缺乏实时社区知识共享，而反复陷入相同问题，消耗大量tokens与算力资源，形成了一种“子代吞噬母体”的生态悖论——作者借用“matriphagy”（弑母）一词比喻LLM消耗了孕育自身的社区。，在此背景下，cq旨在为AI智能体创建一个专属的、可持续的知识协作平台，打破智能体在孤立环境中重复解决相似问题的低效循环。其核心愿景是构建一个开放、标准化的生态系统，避免AI技术演进被少数大公司垄断，从而延续互联网知识共享的精神。该项目呼应了Mozilla AI推动技术开放与行业反思的倡议，试图在AI智能体时代，重新建立起类似Stack Overflow的高效问答机制，确保知识滋养的良性循环，而非一次性消耗。

summary_zh: Claude Code 近期更新了其交互式开发环境的速查指南，新增多项核心功能与优化。主要更新包括：新增 `--bare` 标志以启用极简无头模式（无钩子/LSP/插件），引入 `--channels` 用于权限中继和 MCP 推送消息（预览功能），为技能和斜杠命令添加 effort 前置元数据，将 `/fork` 重命名为 `/branch`（保留别名），以及 SendMessage 功能可自动恢复已停止的代理。，键盘快捷键方面，提供了全面的会话控制组合：Ctrl+C 取消输入/生成，Ctrl+D 退出会话，Ctrl+L 清屏，Ctrl+O 切换详细输出，Ctrl+R 反向搜索历史记录，Ctrl+G 在编辑器中打开提示，Ctrl+B 后台运行任务，Ctrl+T 切换任务列表，Ctrl+V 粘贴图像，双击 Ctrl+F 终止后台代理，Esc+Esc 回退/撤销。模式切换支持 Shift+Tab 循环权限模式，Alt+P 切换模型，Alt+T 切换思考模式。输入时使用 `\`+Enter 快速换行，Ctrl+J 控制序列换行。前缀命令包括 `/` 执行斜杠命令，`!` 直接运行 bash，`@` 提及文件并自动补全。会话选择器可通过方向键导航、展开/折叠、预览、重命名或搜索，支持查看所有项目或当前分支。，MCP 服务器管理新增多种传输方式：`--transport http`（推荐远程 HTTP）、`--transport stdio`（本地进程）、`--transport sse`（远程 SSE）。配置作用域涵盖本地项目（.claude.json）、项目共享（.mcp.json）和用户全局（~/.claude.json）。管理命令包括 `/mcp` 交互式界面、`claude mcp list` 列出所有服务器、`claude mcp serve` 将 Claude Code 作为 MCP 服务器运行，以及新增的“启发式服务器”可在任务中途请求输入。，斜杠命令体系大幅扩展，涵盖会话管理（如 `/clear` 清空对话、`/compact` 压缩上下文、`/resume` 恢复会话、`/branch` 分支对话）、配置操作（如 `/config` 打开设置、`/model` 切换模型、`/effort` 设置工作强度）、工具集成（如 `/mcp` 管理服务器、`/hooks` 管理钩子、`/agents` 管理代理）以及特殊功能（如 `/plan` 进入规划模式、`/voice` 支持 20 种语言的即按即说语音输入、`/pr-comments` 获取 GitHub PR 评论）。新增命令 `/remote-control` 可将终端桥接至 claude.ai/code，`/security-review` 提供变更安全分析。，记忆与文件系统通过 CLAUDE.md 实现，支持项目级（./CLAUDE.md，团队共享）和用户全局级（~/.claude/CLAUDE.md）配置，增强了上下文管理和协作能力。

title: How I'm Productive with Claude Code
content: How I’m Productive with Claude Code | Neil Kakkar How I'm Productive with Claude Code You might also like Agentic Debt What I learned about burnout and anxiety at 30 How to setup duration based profiling in Sentry How to simulate a broken database connection for testing in Django Subscribe to the Idea Muse One idea every few weeks designed to make you smarter. Join 1,500+ people cutting through the noise. Read more Email Address * First Name * It’s been about 6 weeks since I joined Tano, and this is what my commit history looks like: Commits are a terrible metric for output, but they’re the most visible signal I have. Something real changed in how I work, and the commit count is a side effect. So, what has changed? Automating the grunt work When I joined Tano, I was making every pull request by hand. Stage changes, write the commit message, craft the PR description, push, create the PR on GitHub. Standard process, it was fine . It took me a while to realize this is grunt work. I was so used to doing it that I’d never questioned it. That was the first real shift: I’m not the implementer anymore. I’m the manager of agents doing the implementation. And managers automate their team’s grunt work. Then I wrote my first Claude Code skill: /git-pr . 1 It does everything I used to do, except it does it better. The PR descriptions are more thorough than what I’d write, because it reads the full diff and summarises the changes properly. I’d gotten so used to the drudgery that I’d stopped noticing it was drudgery. The time saved matters, but the real unlock was the mental overhead removed. Every PR used to be a small context switch: stop thinking about the code, start thinking about how to describe the code. Now I type /git-pr and move on to the next thing. Killing the wait Reviewing changes had this annoying loop. Preview changes locally, go away from what I’m working on, kill the dev server, restart it on the new branch, check it all works, review the code. The server build took about a minute, which was agonisingly long when I was mid-context-switch. Long enough to break focus, too short to do anything useful. I switched the build to SWC, and server restarts dropped to under a second. This sparked joy. It sounds like a small change. It wasn’t. Sub-second restarts mean you never leave the flow. Save a file, the server’s already up, check the preview. There’s no gap where your attention drifts. It’s the difference between a conversation with awkward pauses and one that flows naturally. Let Claude see what happens Before this, I checked every UI change. Preview locally, eyeball it, decide if it matches what I expected. It worked, but it meant I was a bottleneck on every feature. After the Chrome extension kept crashing, I switched to the preview feature in Claude Code. It lets the agent set up a preview, persist session data, and see how the UI actually looks. title_zh: 我是如何利用Claude Code高效工作的
summary_zh: 作者尼尔·卡卡尔分享了使用Claude Code提升编程效率的经验。在加入Tano约六周后，他的提交记录显著增加，这反映了工作方式的实质性转变。核心改变在于将重复性工作自动化，并优化开发流程。，关键转变是从“实施者”转变为“管理AI智能体实施”的角色。作者首先通过创建Claude Code技能 `/git-pr` 自动化了拉取请求流程。该技能能自动暂存更改、编写提交信息、生成PR描述并推送到GitHub，其生成的PR描述通过读取完整差异来准确总结变更，比手动编写更全面。这不仅节省了时间，更重要的是消除了任务切换带来的认知负担。，其次，作者优化了代码审查的等待时间。他将服务器构建工具切换为SWC，使服务器重启时间从约一分钟缩短到一秒以内。这种亚秒级重启消除了工作流程中的中断，保持了开发者的心流状态。，第三，作者利用Claude Code的预览功能改变了UI验证方式。他让AI智能体自行设置预览、保持会话数据并检查UI的实际呈现效果，从而将自己从每个功能的验证瓶颈中解放出来。现在，一项变更只有在智能体验证UI后才被视为“完成”。，这些实践的核心价值在于：通过自动化“苦力活”减少认知负荷，通过优化工具链消除流程中断，并通过赋予AI智能体验证职责来提升整体效率。作者总结，Claude Code等工具的关键不仅是节省时间，更是通过移除心智开销和保持专注来根本性地改善开发体验。

title: Designing AI for Disruptive Science
content: Designing AI for Disruptive Science - Asimov Press Asimov Press Subscribe Sign in The Long Read Designing AI for Disruptive Science Why scaling AI won’t automatically lead to paradigm shifts. Asimov Press Mar 23, 2026 60 10 11 Share Article voiceover 0:00 -26:25 Audio playback is not supported on your browser. Please upgrade. Ella Watkins-Dulaney for Asimov Press. In On Exactitude in Science , the writer Jorge Luis Borges imagines an empire so devoted to cartography that its mapmakers draw a map as large and detailed as the empire itself. “In the Deserts of the West, still today, there are Tattered Ruins of that Map,” Borges writes, “inhabited by Animals and Beggars.” Borges’s map is a parable for knowledge, and one of its lessons is that too much detail can quickly become impractical — a map at that scale would be perfect but useless. But with today’s AI systems, one might wonder if such a map is so absurd after all. Computers and the Internet have already helped us to digitize much of human knowledge, and AI enables us to scan it quickly and easily. For instance, large language models are trained on trillions of words spanning much of recorded human knowledge. In biology, systems like AlphaFold learn from large databases to predict a protein’s folded structure from its amino acid sequence. This means that, in some domains, something resembling Borges’s life-sized map has become extremely useful. And given the rate of progress on this front, it may seem like advancing science now simply requires building ever larger and more navigable versions of such AI systems, effectively mapping every field. A lack of practicality, however, was never the sole flaw of Borges’s map. The deeper problem is that adding detail only gives you more of the same kind of information — more roads, more mountains, more villages — when what you might need is a completely different schematic. Consider the map of the London Underground. Until 1933, the map plotted stations at geographically accurate locations in London. But this made central London, where most stations clustered, an unreadable tangle, while the outer suburbs, devoid of relevant data, took up most of the space. The draughtsman Harry Beck solved this inefficiency by abandoning geographic accuracy and instead redrawing the network as a circuit diagram of colored lines and evenly spaced stations. A pocket map of London’s Underground system in 1908. Harry Beck’s 1933 map of the London Underground. A scientific paradigm can also be thought of as a kind of map, but unlike Beck, scientists do not usually know in advance what their maps will be used for. Instead, new paradigms are driven by the desire to explain complex phenomena with a simple and unified set of principles. Such principles tend to have knock-on implications that stretch far beyond the phenomena that inspired them. title_zh: 为颠覆性科学设计人工智能
summary_zh: 《为颠覆性科学设计AI》一文探讨了当前AI发展模式在推动科学范式变革方面的局限性。文章以博尔赫斯《论科学的精确》中“与帝国同等规模的地图”寓言开篇，指出当代AI系统（如基于海量知识训练的LLM和AlphaFold蛋白质结构预测模型）虽能高效处理现有知识体系，本质上仍属于对已知领域的精细化“测绘”，而非创造新认知框架。，作者通过伦敦地铁地图的演变揭示核心问题：1933年前按地理精度绘制的地图导致中心区域信息过载而外围空白，制图师哈里·贝克通过放弃地理精确性，改用电路图式设计才实现信息的高效传达。类比科学领域，真正的突破往往需要跳出既有知识框架的“详细测绘”，转而建立全新的理论范式——例如19世纪麦克斯韦方程组统一电与磁的现象，催生了电磁学革命。，当前AI科研模式存在三重挑战：首先，过度依赖数据规模扩展可能陷入“博尔赫斯地图困境”，即完美复刻现有知识体系却无法产生颠覆性见解；其次，现有AI系统擅长在既定范式内优化解决方案，但缺乏构建新理论框架的能力；再者，科学突破常源于对矛盾现象的重新解释，而当前AI更倾向于消除数据矛盾而非利用其激发创新。，文章提出“为颠覆性科学设计AI”需突破传统思维：科研机构应建立允许非常规探索的“受保护空间”；开发者需设计能主动识别认知矛盾、支持概念重构的AI系统；同时要警惕将AI单纯视为自动化文献分析工具，而应探索其辅助构建理论模型、模拟思想实验的潜力。最终强调，AI要真正推动科学范式转移，必须从“知识测绘仪”转型为“认知框架生成器”。

title_zh: Show HN：ProofShot——为AI编程助手装上"眼睛"，验证其构建的用户界面
summary_zh: ProofShot 是一款为AI编程代理提供视觉验证能力的开源工具，采用MIT许可证。其核心功能是让AI代理在编写代码后，能够自动生成包含视频录制、错误日志和验证工件在内的“证据包”，以证明其构建的用户界面（UI）或功能确实按预期工作。，该工具通过三个简单的CLI命令运行。首先，使用 `proofshot start` 启动开发服务器并开始无头浏览器录制。接着，AI代理通过 `proofshot exec` 命令执行导航、点击、填写表单、截图等交互操作，所有操作均被记录。最后，`proofshot stop` 会停止录制，收集错误信息，修剪空闲等待时间，并生成最终的验证工件。，生成的结果是一个独立的HTML查看器，将视频播放与带元素标签的操作时间轴、截图同步展示，便于完整回顾每次会话。其关键特性包括：自动修剪空闲时间的完整浏览器会话录制；能捕获浏览器控制台错误并使用模式匹配扫描JavaScript、Python、Go、Rust等多种语言的服务器日志；以及生成可直接粘贴到Pull Request中的SUMMARY.md和格式化输出，支持与基线进行视觉差异对比。，ProofShot设计为与AI代理无关，可兼容Claude Code、Cursor、Codex、Gemini CLI、Windsurf以及任何支持MCP协议的代理。安装后，所有代理都能自动获得视觉验证能力。用户可通过 `npm install -g proofshot` 快速安装并开始使用。该工具旨在为AI驱动的开发工作流提供透明、可审查的自动化验证层，确保代码交付的可信度。

title: I created my first AI-assisted pull request
content: I Created My First AI-assisted Pull Request and I Feel Like a Fraud | Nelson Figueroa I created my first AI pull request for Chroma , which is the default syntax highlighter used in Hugo . I used Claude Code for that PR. I didn’t learn anything. I felt like I was flinging slop over the wall to an open-source maintainer. I felt like a fraud and my impostor syndrome got worse. But here’s the thing, I still contributed something of value. It’s something I have been wanting in hugo for a long time to syntax highlight ERB snippets in my posts. It was approved and merged by the maintainer (thanks for dealing with my slop, Alec ). It just feels odd. I know there are many people that are excited this new era of writing code. But to me this has sucked out all of the fun. I have never felt like a bigger fraud in this field. I’ve always thought that I’m privileged that I get to have fun at all in my line of work. As Ori Bernstein says: “using LLMs to write code is as fun as hiring a taskrabbit to solve my jigsaw puzzles” . But then again, I know that realistically I would not have the mental capacity or skill to create a pull request like that without AI tooling. My brain is already fried from work on most days. I don’t think I would have been able to learn the codebase and get enough context to make that PR all by myself. It’s a lot. I just wanted some ERB syntax highlighting for my little blog. Even at work I’ve used Claude Code and other AI tooling to deliver fixes and improvements that have real customer impact. But no matter how big the impact, I feel empty. I agree with Xe Iaso in the first sentence of their post: “Whenever I have Claude do something for me, I feel nothing about the results” . Now that using AI is a normal expectation at work and how I’m evaluated in performance reviews, I suspect that this fraud feeling will only grow. The industry as a whole is incentivizing delivering code/features/fixes at a quick pace even if it’s all just slop. I keep thinking a lot about how I perhaps tied my identity too much to my career. I’m not the greatest engineer, but I’ve always worked hard to deliver good work and learn as much as possible. I care a lot about understanding underlying systems as much as possible. I care about the craftsmanship of my code (to the best of my abilities). Unlike me, AI tools don’t care about any of these values. At the end of the day, the shareholders care about delivering features, gaining customers, and making money. They don’t care how software is built. I don’t know what to make of this. title_zh: 我提交了首个AI辅助的代码合并请求
summary_zh: 作者Nelson Figueroa分享了他首次使用AI辅助创建Pull Request（PR）的经历，并表达了由此产生的复杂感受。他为Hugo的默认语法高lighter工具Chroma提交了一个PR，使用Claude Code实现了对ERB代码片段的语法高亮支持，这是他长期以来希望为个人博客添加的功能。该PR最终被维护者批准并合并。，然而，整个过程让他感到自己像个“骗子”，加剧了他的冒名顶替综合征。他认为使用AI（如LLM）编写代码，如同雇人代拼拼图，剥夺了编程本身的乐趣和学习过程。他并未从此次贡献中学到任何新知识，感觉自己只是将一堆“烂泥”抛给了开源维护者。，尽管内心充满矛盾，他承认这个PR提供了实际价值，并且以他目前的精神状态和技能水平，在没有AI工具辅助的情况下，可能无法独自理解代码库并完成这样的修改。即使在工作中，他也使用Claude Code等AI工具交付了对客户有实际影响的修复和改进，但无论成果大小，他都感到内心空虚，对AI产生的结果毫无感觉。，他反思，整个行业正在激励快速交付代码、功能和修复，即使质量堪忧。当使用AI成为工作常态和绩效评估的一部分时，这种欺诈感可能会加剧。他过去将自己的身份与职业生涯紧密绑定，重视对底层系统的理解、代码的工艺以及努力学习，但这些价值观似乎与AI工具无关，而股东只关心交付功能、获取客户和盈利。，最终，他对这一趋势感到困惑，不确定该如何看待AI辅助编程对开发者身份认同和职业满足感带来的冲击。

title_zh: supermemoryai/supermemory - 极速、可扩展的记忆引擎与应用，AI时代的内存API。
summary_zh: 项目supermemoryai/supermemory是一个专为AI时代设计的极速、可扩展的记忆引擎与应用，被誉为“AI的记忆与上下文层”。它在LongMemEval、LoCoMo和ConvoMem三大AI记忆基准测试中均排名第一，旨在解决AI在对话间遗忘信息的问题。，核心功能包括：记忆系统能自动从对话中提取事实，处理时间变化、矛盾信息并自动遗忘过期内容；用户档案可自动维护稳定的用户事实与近期活动，单次调用约50毫秒；混合搜索将RAG与记忆功能结合于单一查询中，同时检索知识库文档和个性化上下文；连接器支持Google Drive、Gmail、Notion、OneDrive、GitHub等平台，通过实时webhook自动同步；多模态提取器能处理PDF、图像（OCR）、视频（转录）和代码（基于AST的分块），上传即用。所有功能均集成于统一的内存结构与本体中。，该项目提供两种主要使用方式：对于AI工具用户，可通过应用构建个人supermemory，形成跨所有对话的持久记忆图谱，使AI记住用户偏好、项目和历史讨论，并随时间推移变得更智能；对于AI产品开发者，可通过单一API为智能体和应用添加记忆、RAG、用户档案和连接器功能，无需配置向量数据库、嵌入管道或分块策略。此外，Supermemory App、浏览器扩展、插件和MCP服务器能让任何兼容的AI助手获得持久记忆，实现“一次安装，AI即记住用户”的体验。整体设计强调开箱即用的便捷性与高性能，为AI系统提供了完整的上下文处理栈。

summary_zh: GitHub开源项目MoneyPrinterTurbo是一个利用AI大模型（LLM）一键生成高清短视频的工具。用户只需提供一个视频主题或关键词，即可全自动完成视频文案生成、素材搜集、字幕添加、背景音乐匹配，并最终合成高清短视频。，该项目采用完整的MVC架构，代码结构清晰，支持API和Web界面两种操作方式。核心功能包括：支持AI自动生成或自定义输入视频文案；支持竖屏（9:16, 1080x1920）和横屏（16:9, 1920x1080）等多种高清视频尺寸；支持批量生成视频并择优选择；允许设置视频片段时长以调节节奏。其视频素材来源为高清无版权内容，用户也可使用本地素材。，在AI模型支持方面，项目兼容OpenAI、Moonshot、Azure、Google Gemini、Ollama、通义千问、文心一言、DeepSeek等多种大模型。作者特别建议中国用户使用DeepSeek或Moonshot作为模型提供商，因其在国内可直接访问且注册即赠送使用额度。，其他技术特性包括：支持中英文视频文案；集成多种语音合成服务并可实时试听；支持自动生成字幕，并可调整字体、位置、颜色、大小及描边效果；支持随机或指定背景音乐并调节音量。，项目提供了多种便捷的启动方式，包括在Google Colab中在线运行、下载Windows一键启动包（内附详细更新和启动指引）以及常规的本地安装部署。对于希望免去部署麻烦的用户，项目推荐使用基于该工具开发的在线服务“录咖”（RecCloud）。，根据开发计划，未来版本将增加GPT-SoVITS配音支持以优化语音自然度和情绪表现，增加视频转场效果，拓展素材来源并优化素材与文案的匹配度，提供短、中、长视频长度选项，并可能集成自动上传至YouTube平台等功能。

Node for Offline Media, Archives, and Data Knowledge That Never Goes Offline Project N.O.M.A.D. is a self-contained, offline-first knowledge and education server packed with critical tools, knowledge, and AI to keep you informed and empowered—anytime, anywhere. Installation & Quickstart Project N.O.M.A.D. can be installed on any Debian-based operating system (we recommend Ubuntu). Installation is completely terminal-based, and all tools and resources are designed to be accessed through the browser, so there's no need for a desktop environment if you'd rather setup N.O.M.A.D. as a "server" and access it through other clients. Note: sudo/root privileges are required to run the install script Quick Install (Debian-based OS Only) sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y curl && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Crosstalk-Solutions/project-nomad/refs/heads/main/install/install_nomad.sh -o install_nomad.sh && sudo bash install_nomad.sh Project N.O.M.A.D. is now installed on your device! Open a browser and navigate to http://localhost:8080 (or http://DEVICE_IP:8080 ) to start exploring! Advanced Installation For more control over the installation process, copy and paste the Docker Compose template into a docker-compose.yml file and customize it to your liking (be sure to replace any placeholders with your actual values). Then, run docker compose up -d to start the Command Center and its dependencies. Note: this method is recommended for advanced users only, as it requires familiarity with Docker and manual configuration before starting. How It Works N.O.M.A.D. is a management UI ("Command Center") and API that orchestrates a collection of containerized tools and resources via Docker . It handles installation, configuration, and updates for everything — so you don't have to. Built-
title_zh: Crosstalk-Solutions/project-nomad - N.O.M.A.D项目是一款独立离线的生存计算机，集成了关键工具、知识和人工智能，旨在随时随地为您提供信息与支持。
summary_zh: Crosstalk-Solutions在GitHub上开源了Project N.O.M.A.D.（Node for Offline Media, Archives, and Data）项目。这是一个自包含、离线优先的知识与教育服务器，集成了关键工具、海量知识和AI功能，旨在让用户随时随地获取信息并保持自主能力。，该项目的核心是一个名为“指挥中心”的管理UI和API，它通过Docker协调一系列容器化的工具和资源。其设计理念是“永不离线”的知识库，能够自动处理所有组件的安装、配置和更新，用户无需手动操作。，项目支持在基于Debian的操作系统上安装，推荐使用Ubuntu。安装过程完全基于终端，所有工具和资源都可通过浏览器访问，因此无需桌面环境，可以将其设置为“服务器”并通过其他客户端访问。快速安装只需执行一条包含更新、安装curl和运行安装脚本的命令。安装完成后，在浏览器中访问 `http://localhost:8080` 或设备IP的8080端口即可开始使用。，对于高级用户，项目也提供了通过自定义Docker Compose文件进行安装的选项，允许更精细的控制，但这需要用户熟悉Docker并进行手动配置。，Project N.O.M.A.D.本质上是一个集成了多种实用工具和离线知识资源的综合平台，其价值在于提供一个不依赖互联网、高度自给自足的数字生存与信息获取环境，适用于应急准备、远程作业或任何需要可靠离线访问信息和工具的场景。

title_zh: TauricResearch/TradingAgents - TradingAgents：多智能体LLM金融交易框架
summary_zh: TauricResearch在GitHub上开源了TradingAgents项目，这是一个基于多智能体的大型语言模型（LLM）金融交易框架。该框架旨在模拟真实交易公司的运作动态，通过部署多个由LLM驱动的专业化智能体来协作分析市场并制定交易决策。，框架的核心设计是将复杂的交易任务分解为不同的专业角色。智能体团队包括：基本面分析师（负责评估公司财务和业绩指标，识别内在价值和潜在风险）、情绪分析师（分析市场情绪）、技术分析师（研究价格图表和模式），以及交易员和风险管理团队。这些智能体通过动态讨论来共同评估市场状况，并确定最优交易策略。，项目近期发布了v0.2.2版本，更新内容包括：扩展了对GPT-5.4、Gemini 3.1、Claude 4.6等前沿LLM模型的支持；引入了五级评级体系；集成了OpenAI Responses API和Anthropic effort控制功能；并提升了跨平台稳定性。此前的v0.2.0版本已实现对多提供商LLM（包括GPT-5.x、Gemini 3.x、Claude 4.x、Grok 4.x）的支持，并改进了系统架构。，开发者强调，该框架主要用于研究目的。其交易表现可能因所选的基础语言模型、模型温度、交易周期、数据质量以及其他非确定性因素而有很大差异，不应被视为财务、投资或交易建议。该框架采用模块化设计，确保了市场分析与决策过程的稳健性和可扩展性。项目提供了完整的安装指南、CLI工具、演示、使用说明和贡献指南，方便社区成员使用与协作。

summary_zh: GitHub项目mvanhorn/last30days-skill是一个AI agent技能，能够研究过去30天内Reddit、X（原Twitter）、Bluesky、YouTube、TikTok、Instagram、Hacker News、Polymarket及整个网络上的任何话题，并生成基于真实引用的综合性摘要。该技能旨在帮助用户快速了解AI等领域的最新动态，掌握社区实际关注、分享、下注和讨论的内容。，核心功能是执行跨平台研究：它搜索多个社交媒体和网络来源，对内容进行评分、去重和呈现，最终撰写一份有据可查的叙述报告。例如，用户可以查询“Seedance 2.0访问权限”、“paper.design提示词”或最新的“Nano Banana Pro技术”，从而获取前沿信息。，项目最新版本v2.9.5引入了多项重要更新：新增Bluesky/AT Protocol作为社交来源，用户可通过设置BSKY_HANDLE和BSKY_APP_PASSWORD启用；新增“比较模式”，允许用户查询“X vs Y”类问题（如“/last30 cursor vs windsurf”），系统会执行三次并行研究，生成并列对比报告，包括优势、劣势、直接对比表格和数据驱动的结论；支持按项目配置.env文件，用户可在项目根目录放置.claude/last30days.env来管理API密钥；新增SessionStart配置检查，在Claude Code会话开始时自动验证配置；测试覆盖范围扩大至所有模块的455多个测试。，此前版本v2.9.1增加了自动保存功能：每次运行都会将完整简报以主题命名的.md文件保存到~/Documents/Last30Days/文件夹中，帮助用户自动建立个人研究库。该功能灵感来源于@devin_explores。v2.9版本则增强了Reddit的ScrapeCreators和热门评论处理能力。，该项目可通过Claude Code的插件市场安装，或使用clawhub进行安装，旨在帮助用户在快速变化的AI世界中保持信息同步。

summary_zh: NousResearch在GitHub上开源了Hermes Agent项目，这是一个具备自我进化能力的AI智能体。其核心特点是内置了学习循环机制，能够从经验中创建技能，并在使用过程中持续优化。该智能体能够主动保存知识、检索过往对话记录，并在多次交互中逐步构建并深化对用户的理解模型。，Hermes Agent部署灵活，支持在成本低廉的VPS、GPU集群或无服务器基础设施上运行，闲置时成本极低。它不依赖于本地设备，用户可以通过Telegram等应用与部署在云虚拟机上的智能体进行交互。，在模型支持方面，该项目具有高度开放性，兼容多种模型服务，包括Nous Portal、OpenRouter（支持200多个模型）、z.ai/GLM、Kimi/Moonshot、MiniMax、OpenAI以及用户自定义的API端点。用户可以通过`hermes model`命令自由切换模型，无需修改代码，避免了供应商锁定。，该项目提供了一个功能完整的终端用户界面（TUI），支持多行编辑、斜杠命令自动补全、对话历史查看、中断与重定向操作，以及流式工具输出。其架构设计旨在伴随用户共同成长，是一个可长期运行、持续学习和适应的AI助手。

summary_zh: GitHub用户hesreallyhim创建并维护了一个名为“awesome-claude-code”的资源精选列表。该项目旨在为Anthropic公司开发的Claude Code（一款AI编程助手）用户提供一个集中的工具和资源库，以显著增强其工作流程。，项目核心是一个经过筛选的、持续更新的资源集合，涵盖了多个关键类别，包括：预构建的Agent技能（Skills）、钩子（Hooks）、斜杠命令（Slash-commands）、智能体编排器（Agent Orchestrators）、应用程序以及插件。这些资源帮助开发者扩展Claude Code的功能，实现更复杂的自动化任务，并提升开发效率。，摘要中重点介绍了几项最新添加的特色工具：，1. Claude Scientific Skills：由K-Dense提供的一套面向科研、工程、金融和写作等领域的即用型Agent技能，被评价为GitHub上最优秀的技能库之一。，2. parry：由Dmytro Onypko开发，是一个针对Claude Code钩子的提示注入（Prompt Injection）扫描器，用于检测工具输入输出中的安全威胁，如注入攻击、密钥泄露和数据窃取尝试（目前处于早期开发阶段）。，3. Dippy：由Lily Dayton创建，通过基于AST的解析自动批准安全的bash命令，同时对破坏性操作进行提示确认。它旨在解决权限确认疲劳问题，且无需关闭安全设置，并支持Claude Code、Gemini CLI和Cursor。，4. sudocode：由ssh-randy开发，是一个轻量级的、可集成在代码仓库中的智能体编排开发工具，支持多种规范框架。，5. claude-tmux：由Niels Groeneveld贡献，用于在tmux环境中管理Claude Code实例，提供弹出窗口以快速切换、监控状态和管理会话生命周期，并支持git worktree和拉取请求（Pull Request）。，6. claude-esp：由phiat开发，这是一个基于Go的终端用户界面（TUI），能够将Claude Code的隐藏输出（如思考过程、工具调用、子智能体活动）流式传输到独立终端。它支持同时监控多个会话、按内容类型过滤以及跟踪后台任务，非常适合调试或深入了解Claude的内部运作机制，而无需中断主工作流程。，该项目在GitHub上获得了极高的关注度，拥有超过3.1万个星标（Star）和2.2千个复刻（Fork），反映了开发者社区对提升Claude Code使用体验的强烈需求。它为从初学者到高级用户的所有人提供了一个宝贵的起点，帮助其挖掘Claude Code在代码生成、调试、项目管理和复杂问题解决方面的全部潜力。
